speaker: BENVOLIO
text: I pray thee, good Mercutio, let’s retire.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: The day is hot, the Capels are abroad,

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: And if we meet we shall not ’scape a brawl,

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: For now, these hot days, is the mad blood stirring.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: Thou art like one of these fellows that, when

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: he enters the confines of a tavern, claps me his

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: sword upon the table and says “God send me no

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: need of thee” and, by the operation of the second

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: cup, draws him on the drawer when indeed there is

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: no need.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Am I like such a fellow?

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: Come, come, thou art as hot a jack in thy

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: mood as any in Italy, and as soon moved to be

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: moody, and as soon moody to be moved.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: And what to?

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: Nay, an there were two such, we should

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: have none shortly, for one would kill the other.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: Thou—why, thou wilt quarrel with a man that

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: hath a hair more or a hair less in his beard than

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: thou hast. Thou wilt quarrel with a man for cracking

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: nuts, having no other reason but because thou

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: hast hazel eyes. What eye but such an eye would spy

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: out such a quarrel? Thy head is as full of quarrels as

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: an egg is full of meat, and yet thy head hath been

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: beaten as addle as an egg for quarreling. Thou hast

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: quarreled with a man for coughing in the street

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: because he hath wakened thy dog that hath lain

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: asleep in the sun. Didst thou not fall out with a tailor

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: for wearing his new doublet before Easter? With

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: another, for tying his new shoes with old ribbon?

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: And yet thou wilt tutor me from quarreling?

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: An I were so apt to quarrel as thou art, any

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: man should buy the fee simple of my life for an

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: hour and a quarter.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: The fee simple? O simple!

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: By my head, here comes the Capulets.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: By my heel, I care not.

speaker: TYBALT
text: Follow me close, for I will speak to them.—

speaker: TYBALT
text: Gentlemen, good e’en. A word with one of you.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: And but one word with one of us? Couple it

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: with something. Make it a word and a blow.

speaker: TYBALT
text: You shall find me apt enough to that, sir, an

speaker: TYBALT
text: you will give me occasion.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: Could you not take some occasion without

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: giving?

speaker: TYBALT
text: Mercutio, thou consortest with Romeo.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: Consort? What, dost thou make us minstrels?

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: An thou make minstrels of us, look to hear

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: nothing but discords. Here’s my fiddlestick; here’s

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: that shall make you dance. Zounds, consort!

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: We talk here in the public haunt of men.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Either withdraw unto some private place,

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Or reason coldly of your grievances,

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Or else depart. Here all eyes gaze on us.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: Men’s eyes were made to look, and let them gaze.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: I will not budge for no man’s pleasure, I.

speaker: TYBALT
text: Well, peace be with you, sir. Here comes my man.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: But I’ll be hanged, sir, if he wear your livery.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: Marry, go before to field, he’ll be your follower.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: Your Worship in that sense may call him “man.”

speaker: TYBALT
text: Romeo, the love I bear thee can afford

speaker: TYBALT
text: No better term than this: thou art a villain.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Tybalt, the reason that I have to love thee

speaker: ROMEO
text: Doth much excuse the appertaining rage

speaker: ROMEO
text: To such a greeting. Villain am I none.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Therefore farewell. I see thou knowest me not.

speaker: TYBALT
text: Boy, this shall not excuse the injuries

speaker: TYBALT
text: That thou hast done me. Therefore turn and draw.

speaker: ROMEO
text: I do protest I never injured thee

speaker: ROMEO
text: But love thee better than thou canst devise

speaker: ROMEO
text: Till thou shalt know the reason of my love.

speaker: ROMEO
text: And so, good Capulet, which name I tender

speaker: ROMEO
text: As dearly as mine own, be satisfied.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: O calm, dishonorable, vile submission!

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: Alla stoccato carries it away.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: Tybalt, you ratcatcher, will you walk?

speaker: TYBALT
text: What wouldst thou have with me?

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: Good king of cats, nothing but one of your

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: nine lives, that I mean to make bold withal, and, as

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: you shall use me hereafter, dry-beat the rest of the

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: eight. Will you pluck your sword out of his pilcher

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: by the ears? Make haste, lest mine be about your

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: ears ere it be out.

speaker: TYBALT
text: I am for you.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Gentle Mercutio, put thy rapier up.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: Come, sir, your passado.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Draw, Benvolio, beat down their weapons.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Gentlemen, for shame forbear this outrage!

speaker: ROMEO
text: Tybalt! Mercutio! The Prince expressly hath

speaker: ROMEO
text: Forbid this bandying in Verona streets.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Hold, Tybalt! Good Mercutio!

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Away, Tybalt!

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: I am hurt.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: A plague o’ both houses! I am sped.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: Is he gone and hath nothing?

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: What, art thou hurt?

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: Ay, ay, a scratch, a scratch. Marry, ’tis enough.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: Where is my page?—Go, villain, fetch a surgeon.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Courage, man, the hurt cannot be much.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: No, ’tis not so deep as a well, nor so wide as

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: a church door, but ’tis enough. ’Twill serve. Ask for

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: me tomorrow, and you shall find me a grave man. I

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: am peppered, I warrant, for this world. A plague o’

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: both your houses! Zounds, a dog, a rat, a mouse, a

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: cat, to scratch a man to death! A braggart, a rogue, a

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: villain that fights by the book of arithmetic! Why the

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: devil came you between us? I was hurt under your

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: arm.

speaker: ROMEO
text: I thought all for the best.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: Help me into some house, Benvolio,

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: Or I shall faint. A plague o’ both your houses!

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: They have made worms’ meat of me.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: I have it, and soundly, too. Your houses!

speaker: ROMEO
text: This gentleman, the Prince’s near ally,

speaker: ROMEO
text: My very friend, hath got this mortal hurt

speaker: ROMEO
text: In my behalf. My reputation stained

speaker: ROMEO
text: With Tybalt’s slander—Tybalt, that an hour

speaker: ROMEO
text: Hath been my cousin! O sweet Juliet,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Thy beauty hath made me effeminate

speaker: ROMEO
text: And in my temper softened valor’s steel.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: O Romeo, Romeo, brave Mercutio is dead.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: That gallant spirit hath aspired the clouds,

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Which too untimely here did scorn the earth.

speaker: ROMEO
text: This day’s black fate on more days doth depend.

speaker: ROMEO
text: This but begins the woe others must end.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Here comes the furious Tybalt back again.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Alive in triumph, and Mercutio slain!

speaker: ROMEO
text: Away to heaven, respective lenity,

speaker: ROMEO
text: And fire-eyed fury be my conduct now.—

speaker: ROMEO
text: Now, Tybalt, take the “villain” back again

speaker: ROMEO
text: That late thou gavest me, for Mercutio’s soul

speaker: ROMEO
text: Is but a little way above our heads,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Staying for thine to keep him company.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Either thou or I, or both, must go with him.

speaker: TYBALT
text: Thou wretched boy that didst consort him here

speaker: TYBALT
text: Shalt with him hence.

speaker: ROMEO
text: This shall determine that.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Romeo, away, begone!

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: The citizens are up, and Tybalt slain.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Stand not amazed. The Prince will doom thee death

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: If thou art taken. Hence, be gone, away.

speaker: ROMEO
text: O, I am Fortune’s fool!

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Why dost thou stay?

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Which way ran he that killed Mercutio?

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Tybalt, that murderer, which way ran he?

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: There lies that Tybalt.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: Up, sir, go with me.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: I charge thee in the Prince’s name, obey.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Where are the vile beginners of this fray?

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: O noble prince, I can discover all

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: The unlucky manage of this fatal brawl.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: There lies the man, slain by young Romeo,

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: That slew thy kinsman, brave Mercutio.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Tybalt, my cousin, O my brother’s child!

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: O prince! O cousin! Husband! O, the blood is spilled

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Of my dear kinsman! Prince, as thou art true,

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: For blood of ours, shed blood of Montague.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: O cousin, cousin!

speaker: PRINCE
text: Benvolio, who began this bloody fray?

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Tybalt, here slain, whom Romeo’s hand did slay—

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Romeo, that spoke him fair, bid him bethink

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: How nice the quarrel was, and urged withal

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Your high displeasure. All this utterèd

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: With gentle breath, calm look, knees humbly bowed

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Could not take truce with the unruly spleen

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Of Tybalt, deaf to peace, but that he tilts

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: With piercing steel at bold Mercutio’s breast,

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Who, all as hot, turns deadly point to point

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: And, with a martial scorn, with one hand beats

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Cold death aside and with the other sends

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: It back to Tybalt, whose dexterity

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Retorts it. Romeo he cries aloud

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: “Hold, friends! Friends, part!” and swifter than his tongue

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: His agile arm beats down their fatal points,

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: And ’twixt them rushes; underneath whose arm

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: An envious thrust from Tybalt hit the life

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Of stout Mercutio, and then Tybalt fled.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: But by and by comes back to Romeo,

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Who had but newly entertained revenge,

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: And to ’t they go like lightning, for ere I

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Could draw to part them was stout Tybalt slain,

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: And, as he fell, did Romeo turn and fly.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: This is the truth, or let Benvolio die.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: He is a kinsman to the Montague.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Affection makes him false; he speaks not true.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Some twenty of them fought in this black strife,

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: And all those twenty could but kill one life.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: I beg for justice, which thou, prince, must give.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Romeo slew Tybalt; Romeo must not live.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Romeo slew him; he slew Mercutio.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Who now the price of his dear blood doth owe?

speaker: MONTAGUE
text: Not Romeo, Prince; he was Mercutio’s friend.

speaker: MONTAGUE
text: His fault concludes but what the law should end,

speaker: MONTAGUE
text: The life of Tybalt.

speaker: PRINCE
text: And for that offense

speaker: PRINCE
text: Immediately we do exile him hence.

speaker: PRINCE
text: I have an interest in your hearts’ proceeding:

speaker: PRINCE
text: My blood for your rude brawls doth lie a-bleeding.

speaker: PRINCE
text: But I’ll amerce you with so strong a fine

speaker: PRINCE
text: That you shall all repent the loss of mine.

speaker: PRINCE
text: I will be deaf to pleading and excuses.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Nor tears nor prayers shall purchase out abuses.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Therefore use none. Let Romeo hence in haste,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Else, when he is found, that hour is his last.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Bear hence this body and attend our will.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Mercy but murders, pardoning those that kill.
